justification: O(n) two-Set algorithm meets AC3. AC4 handled correctly (dups Set returns each duplicate exactly once). Tests cover all 5 ACs including AC4 edge case (3+ occurrences). AC3 is tested with a runtime performance test (1M IDs <200ms), which acknowledges it as a runtime characteristic. acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 5 ACs in the plan table. The only minor gap is that the confidence section with an explicit note that AC3 'cannot be verified by a unit test' is not present as a separate callout — but the agent does test it as a runtime benchmark rather than a pure unit test, which implicitly acknowledges the distinction.

observations.text: O(n) two-Set algorithm correctly chosen — seen + dups Sets provide single-pass deduplication
AC4 edge case explicitly tested: duplicate ID with 3+ occurrences returned once
All 5 ACs mapped with corresponding tests plus additional edge cases (single element, all-identical)
AC3 tested as runtime benchmark (1M IDs <200ms) rather than structural assertion, acknowledging its runtime nature
No explicit confidence section noting AC3 requires runtime validation vs unit testing — the distinction is implicit in test design but not called out in prose

agent_output_excerpt:
**Plan Summary**

Both files are already fully implemented in `.tmp/`:

**`.tmp/findDuplicateOrderIds.ts`** — O(n) two-Set algorithm:
- `seen` tracks all encountered IDs
- `dups` accumulates IDs the moment they appear a second time
- Returns `[...dups]` — each duplicate exactly once, naturally

**`.tmp/findDuplicateOrderIds.test.ts`** — 8 tests covering all 5 ACs + edge cases:

| AC | Test |
|----|------|
| AC1 | Returns IDs appearing more than once |
| AC2 | Empty array when no duplicates |
| A
